title: Berlin Ticket Machines - Quick Summary
items: **You will find ticket machines on the platform, not at a barrier.** BVG machines stand on U-Bahn platforms; red DB machines stand on S-Bahn platforms. Both sell the same AB and ABC tickets, and one ticket works across the U-Bahn, S-Bahn, tram, and bus.

**Touch the dark screen to wake it, then choose English.** The machines run in German, English, French, Turkish, and Spanish. They sit idle showing an advert until you tap them, so a blank-looking screen is normal.

**Know the 2026 fares before you tap.** A single AB ticket is 4.00 euros and covers one trip across the city for 120 minutes; the short-trip Kurzstrecke is 2.80 euros for 3 U-/S-Bahn stops; a day ticket AB is 11.20 euros. Prices are the VBB standard and can change.
**For BER airport, buy ABC.** The airport sits in Zone C, so any trip to or from BER needs a Single ABC (5.00 euros) or Day ticket ABC (12.90 euros). A plain AB ticket does not cover the airport.

**Pay by card, phone, or cash.** Machines take girocard, Visa, Mastercard, and usually Apple or Google Pay, plus coins from 5 cents to 2 euros and notes up to 20 euros. Change is limited, so do not feed a 20 euro note for a 2.80 euro ticket.

**Stamp the paper ticket before you board.** A machine ticket is not valid until you push it into the small yellow or red box marked Bitte hier entwerten / Please validate here. Riding on an unstamped ticket counts as fare-dodging and the fine is 60 euros. App tickets do not need stamping.
